fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong margins and analyst backing, though trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 4.6% (2.37 vs 2.27); next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for current Q (236.6%) and FY (300.7%) are extremely high.
Operating margin 21.8% and Profit margin 17.7% — strong profitability.
Return on equity 22.8% — efficient use of shareholder capital.
Mean analyst target of $1104.68 suggests significant upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 150.52 and Forward P/E of 47.23 indicate a very high valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 26.73 is also very high.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $93.35M is modest relative to market cap.
Beta of 1.53 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Lumentum (LITE) experienced a significant pullback, falling 4% on May 29th as the broader optics sector cooled off, with peers Applied Optoelectronics and Coherent also seeing substantial declines. This comes after a period of strong performance, with LITE reportedly beating Nvidia in year-to-date returns by 7x, reaching 135% YTD. Recent analyst upgrades highlight exploding purchase obligations as a key factor, suggesting strong demand for AI networking solutions, though the stock's high valuation and recent sector rotation warrant attention.
